Topic 7: The operation & explaining federal agents

[163:49]I want to get into this big rally by Patriot Front over the weekend, which I'm sure you've all seen

[164:39]No violence, no drama, no problems, nobody was arrested.

[165:23]they underestimate how difficult that is. And so I commend them. It was a job well done.

[165:35]the logo and insignia of Patriot Front, a white supremacist group were seen protesting

[165:40]in the Eastern Market neighborhood and on Capitol Hill on the 250th anniversary of

[166:20]Here's the thing about this Patriot front group.

[166:25]I think the messaging is basically right.

[166:29]I imagine that I probably agree with the members of this organization on 90% of things.

[167:02]true. I think the people in the organization, hearts are probably in the right place. Like I said,

[167:08]ideologically, mostly sound, let's say. I will also add, I think the charge that they are feds

[167:19]And as long as I've talked about Patriot Front

[167:21]over the years, and I've talked about them many times,

[167:24]I've always said I don't really think the group

[167:27]is a federal operation.

[167:30]I think this is a term that gets thrown around

[167:32]now all the time.

[167:33]Everybody's a fed.

[167:34]I get called the fed.

[167:36]Tucker gets called, I call Tucker a fed.

[167:38]They get called, Alex Jones get called the fed.

[167:41]Everybody gets called the fed.

[167:43]What is the Fed?

[167:45]Well, Fed is short for federal, federal agent,

[167:48]federal informant, that's what it means.

[167:51]And typically we say that in radical politics,

[167:54]we know that our intelligence and law enforcement agencies

[167:57]are very interested in what radical political organizations do.

[168:01]They try to keep them under control

[168:04]because the intelligence and law enforcement agencies

[168:07]are the arm of the Uniparty.

[168:10]And so, in a sense, they want to make sure that we have center right, center left, economic

[168:16]and foreign policy.

[168:18]They want to prevent the rise of an uncontrolled, radical ideological movement on either the

[168:24]right or the left.

[168:26]They also want to prevent violence, terrorism, civil conflict, things like that.

[168:31]And so, law enforcement and intelligence has an interest in penetrating these groups to

[168:39]And so what they will do is they will send an actual law enforcement officers undercover

[168:45]double agents.

[168:47]And they will also take people who have a criminal record and they will get those people

[168:53]to act as informants in exchange for a lighter sentence or negotiating down their criminal

[169:00]charges.

[169:01]So, let's say somebody is in radical politics, they get caught up in some trouble, maybe

[169:06]a gun charge, battery charge, something like this, law enforcement intelligence will come

[169:12]to them and say, listen, we can wipe this slate clean, all you gotta do is keep doing

[169:16]what you're doing and let us know what's going on inside the organization.

[169:19]And they do this because they like to keep tabs.

[169:22]They like to know what's happening.

[169:24]They like the ability to direct or commandeer

[169:26]these organizations at will.

[169:28]They also like to serve as a honeypot for bad actors,

[169:31]people that might want to do violence.

[169:34]Federal agents or informants will try to attract

[169:36]those people to entrap them,

[169:39]and that way they can be imprisoned

[169:40]before they commit acts of violence

[169:42]or something of that nature.

[169:44]That's what that is.

[169:45]And so, when people throw around the word fed,

[169:49]I think we really need to get back to what that actually means.

[169:53]I think that if someone is talking about political violence, that's a sign that somebody's a

[169:58]honeypot operation for the feds.

[170:00]If somebody is really nosy or has some kind of lengthy criminal background.

[170:05]a lot of criminal charges, but maybe not a lot of convictions, maybe mysterious legal

[170:11]problems that go away, things like that, that's grounds for suspicion. There's a lot of things

[170:15]of that nature that you might look at somebody a certain way. Now, I would say about Patriot

[170:21]Front, in terms of their relationship with the federal government, I would certainly

[170:27]doubt that the organization is mostly or run by federal operatives or informants. That

[170:44]penetration.

[170:45]That is just what always happens.

[170:48]The oath keepers, the proud boys, the 3%ers, every radical political organization that

[170:57]is always going to have at least one confidential informant because law enforcement intelligence

[171:02]are just very good at what they do. So it's safe to assume that Patriot Front is probably

[171:07]penetrated. They probably have some level of law enforcement or intelligence penetration.

[171:13]And I would say that's at the minimum. At the most, you might say it's safe to assume
